pages.name: Storage fees? Conveyance charges? Drayage costs? How do you get drayage costs on a pie?

text.content:
This service is unlocked once Dust In The Wind has been completed. It takes place on the Afterlife's roof or in its garage sublevel, and is maintained by Ranger and the Dustwalker clans (or their replacement).

Cost

Depending on the distance, individuals may be transported anywhere in the world for a nominal fee.

See the next section for further details.

  • Night City Transit costs 1 Eagle.

    • This transport usually takes an hour or less.

  • Transport up and down the former USA West Coast costs 3 Eagles.

    • Transport times range from 1-3 days depending on how far north the request.

  • Transport across the Midwest, Southwest Canada, or Central America costs 5 Eagles.

    • Time ranges from 3 days to a week.

  • Transport to anywhere in North or South America costs 7 Eagles.

    • Usually takes anywhere between 5 days to 2 weeks.

  • Trans-oceanic travel costs vary (GM discretion) but start at 10 Eagles.

    • Often takes 3 weeks or more, depending on the destination - oceanic smart-mines necessitate that ships hug coastlines, where nomads and governments endeavor to keep the waterways clear. A trip from Night City to South Africa via Alaska can take 6 weeks or more, and the trip from Night City to Europe can be even longer depending on the season.

  • If the fee is doubled, players may travel by air, reducing travel times by 400%.

Function

The Dustwalkers are a smuggling and transport operation that operate primarily up and down the West Coast of what used to be the United States. Their garage and armory primarily consists of Haulers and modified Generic Compacts, Trucks, and Vans, but also has limited use of A/V-4s and Speedboats.

Their primary selling point is discretion. Aside from Arasaka Counterintelligence, no means in the mook pack are intended to be able to uncover what the Dustwalkers are moving, when they leave, or when they arrive. That doesn't mean transport is always perfectly safe, since raiders are a fact of life, but it guarantees a quiet departure and secure arrival if nothing else.
